creator: shalunov@lynxhub.lz.att.com
text:
Bill Fenner has written:

> No, you're not special; note the past tense in my reply.  In FreeBSD,
> fetch supported proxies and http before ftp did.  In 1994, bsd.port.mk
> used ncftp, which was a pain to maintain.  It switched to fetch in 1996.
> The NetBSD ftp(1) was ported in 1997.

So, wouldn't it be better to use ftp as FETCH_CMD either always or at
least when ftp_proxy is set?  (The latter if fetch is presumed to have
some advantages over ftp.)

-- 
		stanislav shalunov@lynxhub.att.com   |   732-576-3252
